The first loop, the loop gets tighter. The metaphor is not a noose around this neck, so please don't think that's not what I mean. When I say tighter, I mean the language pattern gets more tight because A, you've already pretty much established the first one. The idea that no matter what objection you're presented with, at first it's usually smokescreen, what you just need to circumnavigate around it, then sell your product again, make sure they love it, make sure they like and trust you, make sure they like and trust your company, and then you know with an enormous amount of certainty that you've passed the action threshold, you've stacked up positives, you've knocked off negatives, and basically you're in a position now where you can say with confidence, I can close just about anybody who is closeable. We're gonna talk about the effectiveness and just how to go about doing it. All right, so I think the first thing we need to do, guys, is talk about our sales funnel again. Now, here's how perfect SLM sales funnel. We have a lead capture page, which is also known as an opt-in page or a squeeze page. We know those terms are all interchangeable, and then once they've given us their details in exchange for some kind of an incentive, an incentivized right, so in exchange for a free report or a free massage if you're a massage therapist, or a free clean if you're a dentist, whatever it is that you're offering them for free, it's right there on the lead page, and they said, yes, I want it, here are my details. In the case of a lead gen page, we'd be asking for lots of details, right? We'd ask for the name, first name, surname, we'd probably ask for a mobile number and physical address and so forth, but in the case of a standard lead page or an opt-in form, we're usually just gonna ask for the first name and the email if it's very detailed lead page, it's gonna be first name, surname, and email, alternatively if it's a more crude lead page that just wants to collect as much email address as possible to dump them into the order responder and then lift the straight line email marketing system doers job in getting all those sales, and what we're going to do is just have an email address to element, right? So the lead page will just have an element requiring email address, we don't even need the name because frankly, sometimes the name can lower conversions. It depends on the market, you know, if it's a really sophisticated market and you're selling a real professional service, it helps actually have the name. You'll notice on Jordan's opt-in page, which we went through, I think in module two, we actually have the name and we have the email address and that was because Jordan's involved in a lot of high ticket sales, he's selling and speaking to high level executives, he's speaking around the world, thousands of people, he's approaching, he's being invited to speak at Fortune 500 companies and so forth. So it made sense for us to have a few more elements on that lead page than the standard lead page with a great headline, some good imagery and an email address. And of course, I'm compelling copy encouraging the prospect to submit their details or submit their email address in order to access whatever it is that we're offering for free. Okay, so from that lead page, we're going to go into a sales page. Right now, this particular sales page is an SLO, it's our self liquidating offer, right? This is the sales page, this is an SLO. So it's a low ticket type product, okay? So it's not going to be our call offering, it's not going to be that product that we're really making a whole lot of margin on. This is going to be that product where its primary objective is actually to convert a prospect into a customer once that prospect is a customer, then they can be made an upsell into our core product. So from there, they're going to go into an order form, they're going to submit their details. You know, you can do a clean for free, for example, a teeth clean for free as a lead capture mechanism that could be your lead capture page to free teeth clean. And then you could sell them on something very cheap, like a teeth whitening session. Again, you're not excited about that sale because you're not going to be making any money from there or at least not going to be making much of a margin, right? Because it's a low ticket offer. And then they're going, then you're going to move into an immediate upsell, which is your money maker. So in the case of the dentist holding true to that analogy, so that would be your root canal where you're making all the margins. That's what you wanted from the beginning, but you couldn't just go there and sell a root canal. You couldn't just tell somebody they needed a root canal. You had to give them an examination, you know, and needed to get them into your funnel. And they're going to get into your funnel by being attracted to something that's for free or something that's very cheap. So it's free, okay, free teeth clean. What do I have to lose? And then it's a whitening, oh, a whitening that's only $27. And the dentist across the road is charging, you know, $150. So you'd be crazy not to take advantage of it. As soon as they've exchanged money, as soon as they give you that $27, in exchange for the whitening, excuse me, you know you've effectively converted a prospect to a customer. They become a customer once they give you money. And once the money's left their hand and has been passed over into your cash register, that's it. Now they're a customer. And customers are 20 times more likely to become a repeat customer and buy more things from you than somebody who has never purchased anything from you before. So once they've become a customer, what's the dentist gonna do? He's gonna do his upsell. He's gonna do his upsell for the money maker, the crown in this case. We said crown or we said root canal, whatever it was. So we'll say root canal. And that's where they're making money. That's where they're making the margin. In the case of the dentist, he may not wanna do any additional upsells or downsells unless there's some kind of a monthly continuity that he wants to sell. Like come back every month for an additional clean or whatever that could be an optional upsell. And then finally, it's the thank you page, assuming that it was done online, but typically that's the kind of sale that while it may have started online, the funnel would have progressed to an offline sales funnel. So rather than sending the patients a whole series of emails and web pages, what's more likely to happen is the dentist will be passing them through this funnel while they're on the chair. Or once they've just finished, you know, they're doing an examination and say, okay, it makes sense for you to take advantage of this teeth whitening. And then it makes sense for you to get a crown otherwise or excuse me, get a root canal otherwise. And then you'll solve the benefits of that particular sale. So that's a sales funnel. And the reason we're going over sales funnels again, and I know you guys are probably sales funnel experts by now because I've been circling around this topic over and over again, I keep coming back to it, excuse me. And the reason I do is because it is fundamental to business, it's the single most important thing you could ever do in your business. That's building an awesome sales funnel and understanding why it works. And it's particularly important for what we're doing now. You see, with looping, the objective is to get the prospect to move on to the next step of the sales funnel that they had seen and decided not to take advantage of or not to move forward to or for. You'll see at the bottom of your screen there, it says funnel follow up and there's big blue arrow that runs across. Now that's what we're talking about right now. So the funnel follow up, for instance, somebody has submitted their emails. We're on point one of our sales funnel now, lead capture page. Somebody has submitted their email information on the lead capture page. Then they've moved on to part two, which is the sales page for our self-liquidating offer, for our cheap product, right, our cheap offer. And we've put together an awesome sales page with all of our great copywriting elements that we've gone through, I think in module three, and we didn't get a conversion. We weren't able to progress them to the order form. Okay, they didn't buy. What are we gonna do here? Well, we know after just having listened to Jordan, that there's only three reasons these guys aren't buying. There's only three reasons they haven't moved forward and taken advantage of your self-liquidating offer. And they don't yet like and trust you. They're not totally in love with the product and they don't yet like and or trust your company. We know that. So what we're gonna do here is we're going to use some online marketing technology to reestablish or to establish the three tens based on whichever page the prospect has landed on and then bounced. So in this particular case, we're talking about the prospect landing on our sales page and then bouncing, not making it to our order form. Or they could be on our order form and then they would have seen our upsell because the upsell will come right away after they submit yes, after they submit the form to pay for whatever it is that they purchased on the sales page or self-liquidating offer page. They would have seen our upsell, but they bounced. They didn't move on to the additional upsell. So they seen the core product offering, which is step four on the screen in front of you, but they didn't move on to step five. So we're not gonna let them go just yet. We're going to work on reestablishing or establishing, depending on how good of a job you've done, establishing the three tens up until that point. Or we're going to work on establishing or reestablishing the three tens to make sure that we can close every single prospect who enters our sales funnel that is closeable. If they're closeable, we're gonna close them. And we're going to extract the most amount of value from them, right? That's the whole purpose of the sales funnel. The idea that we can, a customer to us, is worth far more than it is to our customers, to our competitors. Because we've got all of these steps in our sales funnel. We're not only selling them one thing, are we? We're not only selling them, in the case of a dentist, holding onto that analogy. We're not only selling them a crown or a root canal, we said, we're selling them teeth whitening as well. And then we're doing an optional upsell, some kind of a continuity, where we're continuing to sell them monthly whitening or whatever. So a customer to us is worth far more than it is to our competitors. And because the system is so sophisticated and you're able to build a sphere of influence through the lead capture page, and then you're able to make offers continuously to that page, and you have a low ticket offer right at the beginning, which is our self-liquidating offer, which is going to effectively convert prospects to customers. And then our customers are going to be more likely to buy our higher ticket products. You're gonna be converting at a higher rate than your competitors. So right now with this perfect SLM sales funnel that you have in front of you, you're effectively converting higher than your competitors, and you're making more money per customer or client than your competitors, which means that you are well positioned to be able and willing to spend more money in acquiring customers, which means you can grow faster than anybody else in your market. That is the magic of the straight line marketing system. And that's what I really wanna impress upon you guys. If you can do that, if you know that your customer's worth more to you than it is to your competitors, you can spend $10 to acquire a customer. Your competitor can only spend $5 to acquire a customer. Guess what? You can grow as fast as you like. You can take as much market share from them as you feel as you like. You can just absolutely drive into leadership without too much of an effort or too much hassle, and it is absolutely predictable. It's a predictable system, it's reliable, it never fails, you just need to work it. So in this particular case, we're talking about really getting, turning this sales funnel into, really accelerating our efforts, excuse me, within this sales funnel. So building a sales funnel that's really awesome and really kicks us and extracts the most amount of value. And we're gonna do that by making sure we close everyone who enters our funnel, and that is closeable. So if they've made it to our lead capture page, it's time, you know, it's time. You know, we're going to definitely target them. And we're going to make sure we establish those three 10s, and we move them further and further down the sales funnel, further and further down the straight line, until of course we close everybody that's closeable. All right, so let's talk about it. Here is our retargeting recap. We spoke about this briefly now, I believe our traffic module. And I'm gonna go through it again, because this is the basis of what we're going to be talking about, but we just put a real awesome and clever twist on it, and you'll see it in a moment. So here we go, this is how retargeting works. An internet user lands on your site, they're browsing whatever it is they're browsing, and they land on your site. It could be a direct URL insertion. It could have been looking for you. You may have run some offline campaigns, or you may be doing some social media, or they may have heard you're out on the radio, whatever. It may be a direct URL insertion. Alternatively, it may be, they may have responded to an ad. You may be running some traffic. After having done the traffic module, I'm sure you guys are going through all the different traffic options, or you plan on doing that. And you know that you want to test different channels. You may be testing one channel, and you're driving particular traffic. You might be driving Facebook traffic. So they've seen your Facebook advertisement, and they landed on your site. Now, where are you driving traffic? I hope you guys know by now that we're driving traffic to our sales funnel opt-in page. And we're talking about acquiring a customer. So we're talking about acquisition here. We're going to, we need to send them to a mechanism that actually captures the data. So we need to send them to a lead page, or a opt-in page, with a lead capture mechanism there, which in our case is that little opt-in box that requires the visitor to submit their email address. So you've done that, but they haven't submitted the email address. So let's do it that way. They've actually landed on the page, but they haven't submitted their email address. Okay, so you're supposed to have, you're supposed to have converted them at that point because you're supposed to have offered them an awesome free incentive, a free product that acts to incentivize this particular visitor to leave their details, but for some reason they decided not to. Okay, they've left. Now we're on point three. They've left your page. They've decided, not interested, not going to give you my email address. How dare they, right? And then you move, then you put a, then you will go to a retargeting network. And we're going to talk about those networks in a moment, and you give them a whole bunch of stuff, a whole bunch of images, and you set up a particular campaign, and I'll show you what images to give them, and what campaign to set up. You go to that particular network, and you have a few networks to choose from, and you say, if anybody lands on my site, on my particular website, I want you to advertise to them. How do you want them to advertise? Depends on the network. If you go straight to Google, you could advertise on the GDN. Okay, if you go, which is the Google Display Network, if you go straight to Facebook, you can advertise through custom audiences, for example, and you'll get Facebook ads. Alternatively, you can go to third party platforms, which we'll talk about, and they'll advertise your particular ad, you know, across Facebook, across Twitter, across different websites, regular websites, blogs, you name it. So your ad is going to appear everywhere. They won't be able to miss you. So as they land on your site, they're going to see your advertisement. Now here's the thing, they've seen your advertisement. Now, the advertisement captures their interest. Okay, so they're very interested in whatever you have put in front of them, whatever that banner says, was somehow was able to reignite some interest in them. And then finally, that particular user will convert into a loyal customer. What happens, of course, is that user would click on that advertisement that they've seen anywhere on the internet, whether they've seen it on Facebook, whether they've seen it as a banner ad, while they're surfing other websites like Fox News, or CNN, or whatever it is that they're spending their time. They would have clicked on that ad. That ad would send them to, you have two options. You could send them to a particular pre-sell page and then send them to the sales funnel. Alternatively, you can send them straight to a sales funnel, and we'll talk about the difference in a moment. And then of course, the sales funnel does its job, our email marketing does its job, our copywriting does its job, and we therefore are able to march our prospect further down our funnel, thereby extracting the most amount of value from him or her. Okay, so that's how retargeting works. They're surfing the net, they see your ad, they see your website, they click on it. Now they're on your website, they leave your website without taking the particular action that you wanted them to take. You would serve them retargeting ads all around the internet. They see those ads, they click it, they land back on your website, and then they convert into a customer. They've at that point taken the action that they had decided not to take prior to your retargeting efforts. All right, let's talk about how we're going to loop online. All right, so the first thing we're gonna do is strategize. And we've talked about the strategy already, but we'll talk about it in a little bit more detail. Then we wanna create our banner ads. These are the ads that people are going to see as they surf around the internet. So these are the ads that need to spark that interest to get them to click on this particular ad and then land on either a pre-sell page or land straight back into the sales funnel page that they decided not to move forward. And so for instance, if they had submitted their email address, they made it to stage one of opt-in page and it worked, it converted them to stage two, which is where we were presenting them our self-liquidating offer, where they decided not to buy, okay, they've left. So your banner ad will be pushing them or will be directing them either to a pre-sell page, which is just another page that has a whole bunch of ad copy, great copywriting stuff that's going to really sell them to actually take the next step. Or alternatively, you can send them straight to page two, which is the page that they bounced from. So they never made it to page three. They never bought the self-liquidating offer. So in that particular case, you can just put the URL for page two of your funnel as your destination URL when setting up your campaign. We'll talk about it. Then you need to select a retargeting platform. You have countless retargeting platforms that you just from will go through them. I'll show you guys your options. All right, and then from there, after we've selected our retargeting platform, we're gonna create a campaign, which will go through, we'll create a quick campaign together, we'll analyze the results and we'll look at what's winning, we'll keep it and amplify it, which means we'll scale it, we'll scale our winners, our campaigns are doing really well, we'll just turn them up, we'll spend more money on them, we'll cut our losers, we're not happy with the results, we'll cut them, and then it's as simple as wash, rinse, and repeat. So it's that simple guys, so let's get straight into it and talk about strategy. All right, strategy. Now, with respect to the strategy, I want you guys to be mindful of what it is you're trying to accomplish when you get started with your online looping efforts. So by that, what I mean is, the first thing we need to know is where they dropped off from. So for instance, if we know that they purchased our self-liquidating offer, which is stage two of our sales funnel, but they didn't purchase our core product offer, which is stage four, between them, of course, is stage three, which is just the order form, okay? So they purchased that self-liquidating offer, which is that cheap offer that we put online and presented to them with the objective, of course, to convert a K prospect into a customer, but they didn't become actually a high-value customer to us by purchasing that high-margin product, which is our core offering, right? So what's our strategy? Our strategy is really to get them to move from step two to step four, okay? All from step three to step four, because they would have actually completed the order form and will consider the order form step three, just for our purposes here. All right, so we need to get them from three to four. Now we know our objective, okay? So the reason that is so important is because we will actually be setting up our campaign and sending traffic to the page that they didn't convert it. So when we're actually setting up our campaign, they're going through the network, so they'll ask us, okay, if somebody clicks this ad, where do you want them to end up? And if we just approach it haphazardly, we'll just put our homepage for our website, our corporate site, whatever it is, you know, if your business is mybusiness.com, you'll just read our traffic to www.mybusiness.com, which is madness, it's ridiculous. I don't ever want to see you guys doing that because you guys are smart marketers now. Where I want you guys to send traffic is the page that they didn't convert at, that should be your destination URL. So when they say, where do you want that click to go? You need to look at where they'll dropped off. And if they dropped off at the self-liquidating upper stage and didn't convert all the way to your core offering, you want to send them to the core offering. All right, so just be mindful of that. Now the other thing I want you guys to be mindful of is a different strategy, okay? And I don't want to confuse you guys by throwing too many strategies because you have the core strategy down pat and I really want to impress upon you how important that is. The only reason I want to at least mention this particular strategy is because it really works if you've done a poor job in selling one of the 10s, okay? And this is how what I mean. We're going to create a particular ad selling one of the 10s, whether it's your product, yourself or your company. Now, if in your copy or on your opt-in page or on your self-liquidating upper page, whatever page it is that they didn't convert it, if that done a horrendous and terrible job at actually selling one of those three 10s and you know it did, maybe it's you have an opt-in page that you thought it wasn't fitting, you know, you don't want to sell yourself at the opt-in page, you know, it just didn't make sense, right? It just, it seemed off, it seemed a little funny to do that and I've had a hundred situations, thousand situations where that really was the case and I decided not to sell myself at one particular stage of the sales funnel. What you would do is you would have a particular ban ad designed to sell yourself, to try to establish one of those three 10s and then because the banner ad is so small and it might impact the kind of punch that's required to get this person across the line, to get this particular prospect to pass through their personal action threshold, what you would then do in many cases is have another page so rather than landing on your funnel page, the funnel page that they didn't convert it, what you would do is you'll set up a destination URL which is just another page, you can create it in ClickFunnels, it's just one page. You guys know how to create pages in ClickFunnels, one simple page and that particular page actually just acts to further sell so you wanna use your copywriting skills. It further sells yourself because you weren't able to sell yourself in this particular case, in this example. So you have some awesome copy there about yourself, how great you are, how you've changed people's lives, maybe some testimonials, some proof of all the accomplishments you've done, how much you care about your clients and customers or whatever it is you do, it's difficult to speak in these umbrella terms only because I don't know what you guys are doing but assuming you guys are saying professional services, what you're gonna wanna do is talk about all the success you've had in your past, you wanna talk about your track record, how much money you've made with clients in the past, you wanna give testimonials, you wanna do all of that kind of stuff on this presale page and throughout that presale page, you wanna have links there for them to click that's going to push them where? Yeah, to the core offer page, okay, so the core product page, that's the page that they didn't convert it. You know, you've converted them at the self-recruiting offer stage and you convinced that the reason they didn't make it to the next step to your core offering page is because you didn't do a good enough job selling yourself so what you can do, you don't have to do, but what you can do is actually send the traffic to a particular presale page that does more than just the banner so the banner is gonna do some work in terms of selling yourself but you may feel as though it doesn't pack enough of a punch so you want to really hit them with everything in terms of establishing that 10 so you send them to a presale page that's going to do some more heavy lifting with respect to establishing that 10 selling yourself and throughout that page, it's gonna have hyperlinks or buttons or both all the way throughout the page in encouraging the visitor of course to click on it and once they click on it, that URL redirect will redirect to your sales funnel which is of course the page that they hadn't converted at in this particular case, this the core product stage. All right, so there's our strategy, so you have two pieces of strategy there, you have two options, you can of course just redirect traffic, have your destination URL, whatever page you didn't convert at, alternatively you can have a presale page that does a better job at selling one or more of the three 10s and so having done that and you're able to redirect them to that page that they haven't converted at, so it's just like a middle step, all right? Fantastic, so let's talk about the next step which is creating your banner ads. All right, now I've spoken to you guys about banner ads when we spoke about media buying and I instructed you guys to not focus too much on it, right? So what I recommend or instructed you guys to do with respect to your banner ads is to not do your hands with the process, right? No, to just pass it on to somebody else, tell them what you want in it and you can go to fiverr.com and get your banners done for a couple of bucks, literally, you know, you can go to five, 10 bucks, 15 bucks for a whole pack and I can get them to do it. Alternatively, you could go to Upwork and that's Upwork.com, just type banner ads in the search, you know, start, open up an account, type banner ads in the search and people from around the world are gonna bid for the opportunity to create your banners and they do it for next to nothing and they'll create it and you just need to give them instructions. Alternatively, you guys may want to really create your own banners, especially the creative ones among us and, you know, I create banners sometimes, you know, sometimes when I'm feeling a little creative, I'll just build my own banners, put them together and just have some fun with it. So the page they landed at but didn't convert at. The destination URL for this particular ad will be that page. You guys are starting to see the system will come together now. I hope you are because it's exciting. I'd start talking a little faster because I get excited because this system is just so powerful and it just looks like gamebusters. All right, so let's look at the third one. We're gonna sell your company. In this particular case, I didn't use Jordan and I wonder why I didn't. I wonder if you guys will know why I didn't use Jordan. I hope you guys are saying because Jordan, he's his business. Do you remember when we first explored the straight line system in terms of looping? I think it would have been in our fundamentals module, online fundamentals module, I believe. Don't hold me to that though. We said that not in not every case is it appropriate nor should you sell the three tens because there are situations where you don't have a company to sell. You know, Jordan in this particular case with the straight line persuasion system, he is his company. So we sold the product. We sold the straight line persuasion system. We sold Jordan, effectively. Right? There's no company to sell because Jordan is his company. So when somebody buys something or buys a straight line persuasion system, they're not buying it from a branded company but they're buying it directly from Jordan Belfort. So in that particular case, actually we didn't implement any kind of strategy to try to sell a company because there was no company in that case to sell. In some cases, you guys may have a company but you are absolutely the face of your company and people purchase anything from that company. They know they're purchasing it from you as opposed to a larger umbrella brand. In that case, selling your company isn't not only is it not required but it's ill advised. I don't want you guys to selling a company. You're selling a company that nobody knows or cares about. So I want you guys to strategize and realize that there's no one size fits all. So there are a lot of businesses where you don't need to sell yourself, for example. All you need to do is sell your product and you need to sell your company because the actual person who deals with the clients whether it's you or one of your employees, they actually are of little significance to the actual prospect or the customer. It doesn't really matter to them. They don't really care who it is they're dealing with. They just want to deal with a reputable company. The actual person who deals with the transaction or manages the actual specific transaction isn't all that important to them. Think of a bank, for example. If a bank was doing an advertisement, they would want to sell their particular home loan at this particular interest rate and it's brilliant. It's the best home loan ever with the competitive rates and nobody can beat them so on and so forth. So they'll effectively sell the product there and then they'll come back and sell the bank. You know, it's the most trustworthy bank. It's been in business for however long. It's never missed a payment. It's online 24 seven. You can access your money from anywhere around the world because of how robust the network of the bank is and so forth. But what they're not going to do is create another advert selling Rebecca, the account clerk who's going to be managing your deposits. In this particular case, selling a brand, selling the Jordan Belfort brand or the Jordan Belfort company is not all that significant. If we were doing some kind of a corporate deal where Jordan will actually bring in a team of people to actually solo particular or excuse me, train a particular sales force or department or whatever and people are actually buying into the Jordan Belfort brand. We may then run an ad that tries to establish that Jordan Belfort's training company has accomplished all of this. You know, as opposed to Jordan Belfort himself, his company has accomplished all this stuff and will go and sell his company only because in that case, the third 10 becomes relevant but it wasn't in the past. So let's look at one of mine. So this is one of the company that I'm a co-founder and co-CEO of which is click advertising groups. So this is what we've done to sell our company. Somebody who's landed in our sales funnel, okay, didn't make it to the next step and we've effectively already solved the product. We've effectively already sold our selves. In this case, I did sell myself. Again, that could go either way. In professional services, it often does. You know, you need to make an executive decision whether or not it's important to establish that 10, the 10 of course, in selling yourself and weigh that up against not advertising it or not selling it. You know, you need to make a decision. That's often an art. But it's an art that can be guided with a certain amount of science that you need to make that decision. So in this particular case, I did, I sold myself, I sold the product and I sold the product first, tried to establish a 10, I sold myself second, tried to establish a 10 and then third time I was here selling the company. So CAG, the world's only marketing strategy agency that consistently breaks sales records with almost every campaign it executes. You know, and that's an absolutely truthful statement. You know, you have a lot of clients that had a hard time profiting on a particular funnel on the campaign, we'll take it and, you know, and we'll, and it will go game busters, you know, almost always. So we break sales records with almost every campaign that we execute. So that's a statement there that is effectively selling the company, right? So we're trying to establish a 10 on the company. I know that if I can sell my company effectively and I can sell myself effectively and I can sell my product effectively, but what it is I'm trying to sell them. In this particular case, I think it was a local business marketing strategy. Sorry, reengineering some strategy, reengineering. Don't hold me to that. I forgot what the actual product was that we're selling. But I did sell the product, I sold myself and now I'm going in to sell my company. And I know if I can get a 10 in all of those, if they didn't convert, they weren't closeable. I'm only here to close people who are closeable. I'm not gonna waste any amount of energy trying to close somebody who was never going to close. No matter what I did. Okay, and I'm certainly not gonna waste money on it. All right, so that's why we have a frequency cap and as well as the way we have a cap with respect to how many days we're prepared to serve these ads to our prospects or customers. Okay, so there you have it guys. We need to establish a three 10s and that's how we do it. So we have those three banners and those banners are effectively trying to establish one or more of the three 10s. In this particular ad, what I did was I sent them to a intermediary page. We said that we can sometimes send them to a page that sits in between our ad and our sales funnel. In this case, I did. So I was not completely convinced that I had done an effective or efficient job in fantastically selling my company to establish that 10 for the company. So there was an intermediary page, a page that sat in between this ad and the sales funnel. I've done a really good job with really great persuasive copywriting elements throughout that encouraged the visitor to, of course, so would solve the company almost aggressively. Yeah, you really did put a lot of effort in selling the company. And then it encouraged the visitor to click on the link. And once they clicked on the link, they'll be on the page of the sales funnel that they had since dropped off and not converted it. All right, so the other kind of banners, if you guys are gonna build them, go into Bannerstack and build them. If you're going to send them off to a designer, excuse me to develop, you can use these samples. Okay, you can say, I want an ad that looks like this, but you obviously would change the copy. So, you know, whatever it is you do, put your logo, you can use the skyscraper banner that you had just seen and put your logo and say, I kind of want it to do this. Ideally, don't let, here's another tip. Don't let designers write your copy. You write your copy. You write the words that are gonna be on the banner. You only have them design it. Let them be responsible for the pictures. Don't let them touch words. Never, ever, please. Don't say, I just want a banner that's going to do a good job of selling my company or a banner that's going to be, you know, instructing people or identifying the, this particular accomplishment that I've done. And that's of course gonna be a banner that's gonna be selling yourself. And just leave it up to the copywriter to create the actual copy. Cause I promise you they're gonna come back with, you know, one line headline is gonna be horrendous. It's gonna be awful. It's not gonna convert. There's a good chance they're only just gonna come with the company name and then some nice imagery to camouflage their poor copywriting efforts. So you guys write the copy. You tell them I want this amount of words on this particular banner and they'll tell you whether it's aesthetically pleasing or not. You can see this particular banner is pretty crammed. You know, so you may not want to include as many words on this particular, on a banner this size. Now this banner's been blown up. Okay. This is a standard square banner. It's been blown up. So you guys can see it on the screen, but it's actually much smaller. And when it does shrink down to the actual live size, it can be a little small. And some people may not be comfortable having all that kind of copy, all those words on such a small banner. Speak to your designer about that. Make sure it's aesthetically pleasing and it's going to convert well. But again, you're giving them the responsibility to design the banner, not to actually write the copy that's going to be on the design. That's your job. You're the strategist. You need to know that I need to sell my company. So you're gonna write some words that sell your company. I need to sell myself. You're gonna write some words to sell yourself. And I need to sell this product. You're gonna write some words to sell, to do just that. And you know, once you establish a 10, 4, or 3, you're gonna close everybody that's closeable. That's when things get exciting. All right guys, so the next section is we're going to talk about selecting a network. So we've done our strategy. Okay, we know exactly what we're trying to accomplish. And we've done our banners. We've designed all these core banners and we have a series of banners that's gonna sell our souls. We have a series of banners that are gonna sell our business. We have a series of banners that are gonna be selling our product. And they do a great job in establishing the three 10s. Now, the next thing is we need to serve these ads to everybody online. Okay, so now we need to select a network. So looping online is just as simple as buying traffic online where it doesn't, the actual process is not that difficult. The only challenge is actually finding where to go. As long as you know where to go, traffic is easy, right? You just go to the network and you give me a credit card. In this particular case, it's the exact same thing. We need to just find out what platform we're gonna use to serve our ads to people who have been on our website before. In this case, we can't just go to a regular traffic platform. We need to go to a special traffic platform and that's a platform that has the capability, excuse me, to serve ads to people who have been on our website before. And those platforms are called retargeting platforms. Let's take a look at them. So here, retargeting discovery platforms. So let's look at all the different retargeting discovery platforms we can pick from. Now, this is actually shy a few, there are a few more options, but like I said, if I'm not familiar with it, I won't recommend it. I can't do that in good conscience. These all are great. I have no issues with any of these. I love them all. Okay, Google AdWords. You can go straight into the Google AdWords manager, okay, you can go straight into your account and then you can actually retarget people who have been on your site through Google AdWords. You can do it directly through Facebook. Facebook custom audiences, anyone who's landed on your page, you can send them a particular ad to encourage them to take the next step in your sales funnel. You can do it through another key cast platform called Perfect Audience. The good thing about Perfect Audience is rather than having to do it on Facebook separately, you can actually, or Twitter separately or whatever, you can actually just do it all on the Perfect Audience platform. Just put your ads up on Perfect Audience and they'll serve it to Facebook and they'll serve it to the rest of the web and they'll serve it all around the internet. Your prospect can't miss you, no matter how hard they try, by doing it through a third party network like Perfect Audience. Ad role, retargeter, do the exact same thing, it's Perfect Audience and trigger. They all function as third party platforms where they will serve your ads all around the internet, even through websites like Facebook, depending on their relationship with that particular channel, and they'll serve your ads everywhere. So you can just use one third party platform, not worry about the rest. Now here's the thing though, I've decided to go through Perfect Audience. I think we'll get the most bang for our buck if we go through Perfect Audience. But like I said, I've been through one, you've been through all, they're actually pretty easy, but I do wanna give you guys a feel of how it all works. Now here's the thing about Perfect Audience, it's good, it's great, I like it, but it does have a 1000 cookie minimum. I believe they still have 1000 cookie minimum, we'll go live and we'll have a look at it together, but what that means is prior to serving your ad to other people, you would need to have cookie, you need to have 1000 cookies, you need to have 1000 visitors at your website, who have clicked onto your website. Now for some of you, you're gonna scoff at that and laugh at that, just maybe gill it all. You're not really concerned, that's a waste of your time, because you're getting 1000s and 1000s of visitors, and it's not an issue. For others, you may say, oh, 1000 visitors is quite a lot. I've only had two or 300 visitors in the last month, so I'd have to put a cookie in it, which is cookies, just a badge, remember guys, it's just that badge we're gonna pin a badge onto our website, and anyone who's been on it is going to be notified, we're gonna be notified, excuse me, we're gonna know they've landed on our website, and in this particular case, the script or the pixel is going to be provided for by a perfect audience, we're gonna put that onto our final page. And you may say, okay, well, that's a little too much, so we'll, you know, it's totally up to you, so you can go straight to Facebook, for example, and not have to worry about that, not have to worry about that 1000 visitors. But we'll go through a perfect audience only, because I wanna show you guys, because it is a little bit more versatile, and it does allow us to advertise on different channels, so if we just go through the Facebook custom audience, all you guys are gonna see is ads being served on Facebook, but if we go through a third party, like perfect audience, you guys are gonna see how easy it is to serve ads on Facebook or serve ads on blogs and news sites, and all over the internet, so our prospects simply can't escape us until we've established our three 10s, thereby closing the sale, or at least closing anybody who is closeable. All right, guys, so let's take a look at perfect audience interface. Let's do it. All right, guys, here we are in the perfect audience backend interface, so what I've done is I've just started a free trial, and from there, you'll also get $100 credit, which is good, and I think every trial lasts for 15 days or so, so I've just started a little dummy account to show you guys around and show you how it works. All right, so the first thing I want you guys to do is to just take a look at the policies, make sure there's no issue with whatever you're selling, scroll down, quality guidelines. Okay, advertiser quality guidelines, so here's the thing, guys, you can spend so much effort and time creating a real kick-ass campaign, or some sales funnel, you've got your copy down, it's perfect, you've got some great ads, great banner ads, and so forth. You run the ad and you may be running it with some success, and then it gets flagged for some reason, or it gets blocked before it can even run, because it violated one of these guidelines, and the worst part about that, of course, is that if you get flagged enough, or if you get flagged for a serious enough offense, they should kick you off the network, so you can't advertise on the network ever again, and that's pretty painful for advertisers and marketers like us, so just be mindful of this, make sure you've satisfied the requirements, and you don't breach any of the guidelines, so no adult products, no affiliates, you can't sell anybody else's product through this particular platform, no alcohol drugs, tobacco, e-cigarettes, and steroids, clarity and unverifiable claims, don't tell anybody or don't list a product that says it cures cancer, because it does not, or at least it can't be verified so don't write it. Blogs and information sites, you can't send traffic to a site that's not selling anything, there's just a whole bunch of information, some blog, some blog about you or about your travel, so whatever, you can't actually send retargeted traffic to a site like that, dating, matchmaking, so forth, no exit pops, exit pops of course, you all know are the annoying little pop-ups that come up once somebody tries to exit a page, now just be mindful that while exit pops are not allowed on the destination URL, they're not gonna stop you, or they can't stop you, I believe, from actually putting an exit pop on anywhere else in your funnel, so on your destination URL, the page that you're actually gonna send the traffic to, make sure you don't have any exit pops, but on the following page, you can do whatever you like. Facebook news feed, financial products, no gambling, and no redirects, make sure you have a terms and policy on your terms and privacy policy, excuse me, on your page, no need to make it so overt like some people do, that half the screen pop up with a message saying that you're likely to be cookie by visiting this site, do you accept or not, and you need to click yes to view the content, no need to do anything so obvious is that, frankly, I don't know why anybody does that, but just make sure you have a little link on your website, a terms and privacy policy link, and have it noted there that you may cookie them. A cookie, of course, means that badge that we're talking about, it's gonna badge that website so you know if somebody's visited it. Now, weapons and explosives, weight loss products, and it looks like that, was it? Weight loss products and pills are prohibited. All right, now, how are we going to track and retarget people, that's wearing method now, so you can do it path based on what page they've visited, so you can write yourdomain.com forward slash, you know, then you can write your SLO page, and then you could write, this is the URL, the SLO URL, or the core product URL, or the upsell URL, whatever it is, the URL that you're actually planning on retargeting, you'll just put that there, okay, so it's as simple as that, so if they visited the SLO and you wanna run a campaign to solve them the SLO, because you know that they hadn't purchased the core product, then you can actually just put the SLO URL there, and we can follow them around online until they purchase the core product, and we can satisfy or establish those three 10 to move them down our sales funnel. Alternatively, you can just do the whole site, which of course I don't recommend, but just for instruction purposes, excuse me, you can just pull this wild card here, and that's going to target the entire domain, all right, so anyone who's lands on any page of our website is going to be retargeted. All right, the other one is query string, query string is based on the word on the, in the URL, so for instance, you could have your URLs named in such a way that you know exactly how to track them based on the name, so for instance, anybody who lands on any of the checkout pages of a particular website, you may have the word checkout in the URL, okay, so you say, okay, anyone who's landed on their checkout page, I want to retarget them, that means I want to retarget anybody who's landed on a URL with this word, and in this case, this word is the word checkout, okay. Now, the next one is event, event is interesting, event basically, those of you who are familiar with Google Analytics and goals, and metrics, key metrics, and micro metrics and all that stuff, so basically, if somebody takes some kind of an action on your website, for example, they submit a form or they open up the live chat, or they click a video to watch, or they click any particular button or hyperlink, whatever, they take any kind of action on your page without even having to go to another page, you can actually retarget them based on the action they've taken, so that's how granular you can get, so it's a lot like Google Analytics in that respect, so you can just target just about anybody who's done just about anything on your website, through that particular method, and the other one which is interesting is email, you can track visitors who open an email, so that's pretty awesome because you can send, say, you need to use your SLM email marketing skills, send an All right, so here is the dashboard by the way, so once you've actually completed it, and you want to analyze your results, just go into dashboard and just take a look at what's happening, so you look at how many impressions were served, that means how many people have seen the ads, how many clicks they got, okay, then the CTR, clicks are right, you guys are familiar with these terms now, right? So we went through them in our fundamentals, and you'll have them in your terminology sheet sheet, excuse me, impressions, remember that how we spoke about CPM, which is the thousand impressions, how much it cost per thousand impressions, spoke about click through rate, which is the amount of people who have seen the ad, compared against the amount of people who actually have clicked the ad, so they're all there, right, and then you're gonna have how much it costs you in total, you're gonna have your conversion rates, how many people have actually accomplished the goal that you've set, you're gonna have your budget there, your revenue, how much money you've made, and like you guys know, for the last few steps of our campaign process, for our retargeting campaign, is to analyze the results, we scale the winners, meaning we invest more, the actual ads that are working really well for us, the campaigns that are working really well for us, and we cut our losers, we wash and we rinse and we repeat, and that process is done through the dashboard, where you analyze all your results, and just be sure, because I've made this mistake before, the only reason I'm telling you guys, just be sure you have your actual time parameters set correctly, sometimes I'll have, say, the last seven days for the last week, and then look when I'm a week later, and I'll be shocked that it was the exact same results as last week, and then I'll remember that I hadn't changed my date settings, or my time parameter, okay, you can set a custom range, as well, so between this date and that date, I wanna see the results, you know, between the sevens and the 13th, I'd like to see these results, just be mindful of that, keep in mind, make sure it's on the right date before you decide to scale a campaign or cut a loser. You see, straight line marketing is all about building up the positives, knocking out the negatives, and changing your customers beliefs about buying all at the same time.
